resolvedQuestions: What exactly do the shipped command routers, presentation assets, and shared creation contract contain, and which literal-prompt qualities are absent?
What include/shared-fragment mechanism does the runtime support?
What complete literal body should each of the five commands contain?
Which content stays literal, shared, or mode-owned?
What file/change/test sequence can ship the rewrite safely?
What exact project-root include spelling should implementation promote?

keyFindings.summary: The five shipped bodies are substantial mode routers, not self-contained creation prompts.
Command-specific intake, sibling discrimination, stable mappings, and typed products are working architecture worth preserving.
Presentation assets implement progressive intake and visible lifecycle blocks but fragment prompt wording away from command bodies.
The skill-local creation contract centralizes choreography and authority while intentionally excluding design taste.
The remaining gap is an evocative, actionable, command-visible creation brief, not missing routing architecture.
@./path is the smallest direct runtime file-content expansion mechanism; it makes the rendered prompt, not the checked-in command bytes, self-contained.
$ARGUMENTS preserves invocation intake but is not a shared-fragment mechanism.
Shell-output rendering is feasible but introduces disproportionate registry, drift, rollout, security, and manifest machinery.
Generation is required only if every checked-in command must contain all canonical shared bytes.
Use a literal mode-specific creation brief plus one @ reference to the shared normative creation contract.
Literal command bodies should own the mode-specific mission, progressive intake, ordered work, critique rule, evidence ceiling, and typed product while delegating taste.
One canonical @ expansion carries shared lifecycle and schemas without five-copy drift.
A three-way placement rule separates literal public orchestration, shared normative schemas, and mode-owned judgment.
All five bodies can share one structural grammar while retaining distinct decisive quality rules.
Design-reference substitutes source fidelity and provenance for anti-generic creative originality.
Keep mode-specific missions and local fields, but move universal lifecycle, intake schemas, output blocks, proof labels, and handoff continuity to the shared contract.
The proposed OK|DEFER|FAIL shorthand regresses the required STATUS=ASK path.
Suffix behavior and prompt ownership must be changed atomically because literal wrapper prompts conflict with current presentation-owned wording.
Audit remains review-only; md-generator alone may write artifacts and only measured values may enter token tables.
Safe shipment requires an include-root fixture, test-first body invariants, atomic package ownership edits, and suffix/status/mutation negative cases.
Promote @.opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/creation-contract.md; OpenCode 1.18.4 accepts leading-dot names and resolves them from ctx.worktree.
Final bodies combine literal public orchestration with exactly one shared lifecycle/schema expansion.
Acceptance is package-wide and locks statuses, evidence ceilings, mode boundaries, audit read-only behavior, and md-generator fidelity.
Wrappers, presentation and suffix fixtures, and metadata ownership must change atomically.
The end-to-end sentinel remains the first implementation gate, not an unresolved spelling decision.

ruledOutDirections: Copy mode-owned taste doctrine into commands
Treat presentation assets or the shared contract alone as the literal-prompt deliverable
Use Markdown links or read imperatives as loader includes
Use a shell-command renderer as the smallest mechanism
Copy mode-owned reference inventories or transport mechanics into commands
Apply anti-generic creation rhetoric to source-faithful extraction
Keep presentations as a competing normative prompt authority
Allow brief-provided values into design-reference token tables
Claim exact @./ rooting without source evidence
Accept @./.opencode/... as a second canonical spelling
Treat the include sentinel as an unresolved architecture decision
